salesforcedx-vscode-manager.description: Manage which Salesforce VSCode extensions are enabled, verify external dependencies, and override with local VSIX builds.
salesforcedx-vscode-manager.configuratio: Salesforce Extensions Manager
User-defined extension groups (merged with built-ins by id).
What to do with managed extensions not in the applied group.
How apply-group changes workspace extension state.
Use the `code` CLI to install/uninstall extensions. Applying a group with scope=disableOthers uninstalls non-members.
Each group maps to a VSCode Profile; applying a group switches profiles (coarse-grained but cleanly isolated). Reserved for a future release.
Run dependency checks automatically on startup.
Non-Salesforce extensions the manager may enable/disable as part of a group.
Absolute path to a directory of local .vsix files. When a managed extension has a matching vsix, it is installed from there instead of the marketplace.

Automatically install every .vsix found in the VSIX directory at activation and whenever a file is added, changed, or removed. Overrides are authoritative — the Groups view hides install/uninstall/update actions for VSIX-backed extensions so the override directory stays the single source of truth.
Show the active group in the status bar.
How to handle the 'reload required' signal at the end of a group apply. Consolidates VSCode's per-uninstall reload banners into a single prompt.
Automatically reload the window after a group apply finishes if any extensions were installed or uninstalled.
Show one consolidated reload prompt after a group apply instead of VSCode's per-extension reload banners.
Never prompt; rely on VSCode's built-in per-extension reload banners.
Show the VSIX override indicator in the status bar when a vsix directory is set.
When the manager should probe the VSCode Marketplace for newer versions of managed extensions. Results are cached in memory for ~1 hour.
Probe the Marketplace for newer versions of managed extensions shortly after activation.
Only check when the user explicitly invokes the 'Check for Extension Updates' command or expands the tree after a refresh.
Never contact the Marketplace; only show the locally installed version.

Controls whether the Salesforce Extensions Manager emits its own telemetry events. Honors the global `telemetry.telemetryLevel` setting first; this flag is an extension-specific opt-out for users who want manager events suppressed without disabling VSCode telemetry entirely.
Get Started with the Salesforce Extensions Manager
Switch between extension groups, verify dependencies, and swap in local VSIX builds.

Quickly enable the set of Salesforce extensions you need for the task at hand.
[Apply a Group](command:sfdxManager.applyGroupQuickPick)

See which external prerequisites (Salesforce CLI, Java, Node) are healthy.
[Check Dependencies](command:sfdxManager.runDependencyCheck)

Point at a folder of .vsix files to test unreleased builds without leaving the marketplace install for production.
[Open VSIX Setting](command:workbench.action.openSettings?%22salesforcedx-vscode-manager.vsixDirectory%22)
The status bar shows the active group and any VSIX overrides; click either to manage.
salesforcedx-vscode-manager.viewsWelcome:
Loading Salesforce Extensions Manager…

Apply a group to enable the extensions you need, install or update individual extensions, or browse the full Salesforce publisher catalog.

No VSIX overrides present.

Drop a `.vsix` file in the configured VSIX directory and it will auto-install; the matching row in the Groups view becomes locked so the override directory stays the single source of truth.

Loading Salesforce Extensions Manager…

Verify that the Salesforce CLI, Java, Node, and other prerequisites needed by the managed extensions are installed and on PATH.
